## **12-STEP LIFE PLAN WITH OBSTACLE ELIMINATION SYSTEM**

**IF YOU WANT TO CREATE A COPY OF THIS FILE ON YOUR GOOGLE DRIVE:**  
 **Click “File” \> “Make a copy” \> “Make a copy”**

## **BEFORE YOU BEGIN: READ THIS**

**Welcome to your 12-Step Life Plan.**

**This is not a stiff, military checklist. It’s a starting point — a launchpad — and a system designed to help you create unstoppable momentum, not to trap you in perfectionism.**

**Think of it like a car / boat:**  
 **You can’t steer a car that isn’t moving.**  
 **If you wait until you “feel ready,” until you “have it all figured out,” or until life is perfect — you’ll stay stuck.**

**![][image1]![][image2]![][image3]**

**Start moving, even if it’s messy.**  
**Start planning, even if it’s rough.**  
**Start acting, even if your brain screams “not ready.”**

**This plan is here to get your wheels turning. Once you're in motion, you can adjust. You can refine. You can pivot. But first — get rolling.**

---

## **THIS SYSTEM IS A TOOL, NOT A CAGE**

**The 12 steps will help you clarify your vision, take powerful daily actions, eliminate obstacles, and stay on track. But remember this rule:**

**Play around your nature.**

**Everyone’s energy, rhythm, and motivation is different.**  
 **Don’t copy someone else’s routine. Don’t beat yourself up for not being a robot.**  
 **Instead, study your own patterns. Build self-awareness. Adapt the system to work for you.**

**If you’re a night owl, plan for it.**  
 **If you lose focus after 2 hours, work in short sprints.**  
 **If you need frequent change, rotate your environment.**

**This system works when you respect how *you* work best. That’s how you make real, sustainable progress.**

---

## **HOW TO USE THIS PLAN**

1. **Print it out or copy it into your own digital journal or tool.**

2. **Work through each step, one at a time.**

3. **Be honest. Brutal clarity beats fake motivation.**

4. **Review and adjust weekly.**

5. **Focus on action, not perfection.**

**If you stumble, restart.**  
**If you feel stuck, simplify.**  
**If you lose track, just return to your dream.**

**Use additional tools: AI models: chat gpt, gemini, grok etc., our group, our consultations, free or paid tests / investigations, free consultations with experts, seek help**

---

## **MOST PEOPLE WON’T DO THIS**

**Most people drift. Most people break promises to themselves.**  
 **But you’re choosing to take control. You’re choosing to face distractions, doubts, and delays head-on.**

**This plan won’t do the work for you. But it will give you the structure, focus, and mindset to do the work that matters.**

**Let’s build the life most people only talk about — one focused day at a time**

---

1. **CLARIFY YOUR DREAM (VISION LOCK)**  
    **Visualize the life you want to live in 5 years.**  
    **Where do you live?**

**What does your ideal day look like from morning to night?**

**How do you feel in your body, mind, and relationships?**

**What are you most proud of having accomplished?**

**Write this as if it already happened.**  
 **“It’s May 5, 2030\. I wake up in \_\_\_\_\_\_. Each morning I \_\_\_\_\_\_. I feel proud because \_\_\_\_\_\_.”**

---

2. **DEFINE IT CLEARLY**  
    **Turn the dream into measurable goals:**  
    **Health & fitness (e.g., run 10km, lose 10kg, gain muscle)**

**Learning & skills (e.g., speak Italian, master design, etc.)**

**Finances (save X amount, become free from debt)**

**Environment (move to \_\_\_\_, declutter, build ideal space)**

**Experiences (travel, learn to dance, climb mountains, etc.)**

**Tip: Use the SMART format: Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound.**

---

3. **IDENTIFY CORE ACTIONS**  
    **What daily/weekly habits are necessary to achieve your dream?**  
    **Examples:**  
    **Wake up early at 6 AM**

**Read for 30 minutes a day**

**Exercise 5x per week**

**Meditate 10 minutes a day**

**Study/practice new skill 60 minutes per day**

**Create a short daily routine checklist with 3–6 key actions.**

---

4. **CREATE YOUR DAILY EXECUTION PLAN**  
    **Break down your yearly goal into months → weeks → days.**  
    **Define what progress looks like this month.**

**Define what needs to happen this week.**

**Know what to do today.**

**Use a habit tracker, calendar, or simple to-do system.**  
 **Your job is to win today. Every single day.**

---

5. **RUN A "PRE-MORTEM": WHY MIGHT YOU FAIL?**  
    **Imagine it's 2030 and you failed. Be brutally honest: Why?**  
    **Common answers:**  
    **I got distracted by my phone**

**I watched too much YouTube/Netflix**

**I didn’t believe in myself**

**I quit when it got hard**

**I didn’t follow my plan**

**I kept restarting new goals instead of finishing one**

**Write a full list of reasons. Then write:**  
 **“How will I prevent each of these?”**  
 **(This is your first obstacle elimination system.)**

---

6. **PREPARE FOR WAR: OBSTACLE ELIMINATION PLAN**  
    **For every goal, create:**  
    **Distraction shields (phone apps blocked, workspace cleaned)**

**Time blocks (no interruptions allowed)**

**If-then fallbacks (e.g., “If I miss the gym, I will do 100 pushups at home”)**

**Treat every day like a mission behind enemy lines. You either execute or make an excuse.**

---

7. **BUILD YOUR MENTAL FORTRESS**  
    **Install mental conditioning practices:**  
    **Journaling every night**

**Cold showers / short discomforts**

**Meditation or breathwork**

**Affirmations of identity: “I am someone who finishes what they start.”**

**This trains you to push through doubt, delay, and fear.**

---

8. **INSTALL DAILY DISCIPLINE BLOCKS**  
    **Use non-negotiable work blocks (e.g. 8–10 AM \= deep work).**  
    **No phone**

**No social media**

**Timer on for 90 minutes**

**Focus on one single task**

**Use apps like Forest, Cold Turkey, or Freedom to block distractions.**  
 **Start every day with your hardest/highest-leverage task.**

---

9. **DO WEEKLY REVIEWS**  
    **Every Sunday, reflect:**  
    **Did I follow my daily actions?**

**What slowed me down?**

**What was a win?**

**What will I change next week?**

**Keep it in a journal or Notion doc. Progress without feedback \= drifting.**

---

10. **RESET YOUR ENVIRONMENT**  
     **You can't grow in a space that pulls you down.**  
     **Clean your room/desk**

**Create a space for deep work**

**Reduce noise, clutter, toxic triggers**

**Remove junk food, install healthy snacks**

**Surround yourself with visual reminders of your goals**

**Control the environment → Control your behavior.**

---

11. **GUARD YOUR ENERGY & IDENTITY**  
     **Sleep 7–8 hours**

**Don’t waste willpower on decisions: automate routines**

**Limit inputs: remove junk information (doomscrolling, bad news, gossip)**

**High performance comes from consistency \+ recovery.**

---

12. **MAKE YOUR MISSION UNAVOIDABLE**  
     **Set internal consequences: “If I skip this, I do X pushups.”**

**Tie identity to behavior: “I’m the kind of person who trains daily.”**

**Put your goal in front of you: sticky notes, lockscreen, mirror notes.**

**Burn the escape routes. This is your time.**

---

**BONUS:**

**WEEKLY OBSTACLE REFLECTION (USE THIS TEMPLATE)**  
**Every Sunday, write this in your journal:**

 **What slowed me down this week?**

**What was my biggest distraction?**

**What am I proud of?**

**What will I eliminate/change this week to go faster?**
